name: Anthony's

notes: Anthony's current restaurant-issued Owner menu publishes 178 category presentations across 20 categories, canonicalized to 175 products after merging only Garidomakaronada, Greek Fries, and Meatball repeats.

All 184 frozen rows were reconciled: 152 exact matches, fourteen spelling/presentation variants, and eighteen nested modifier or heading artifacts. The 166 matched products leave nine current omissions.
The repair retains all 175 current top-level products, including the nine restored rows, and preserves the current official menu and item URLs.

The current restaurant-issued menu publishes product names and ingredient descriptions but no complete allergen matrix or cross-contact disclosure. One hundred twenty-nine products have direct positive signals; forty-six remain accurately unavailable.

Eighty matched frozen allergen outcomes differ from the current product and ingredient text. The repair corrects omitted pasta, bread, dairy, fish, shellfish, and tree-nut signals without treating silence as safety.
The generic raw-or-undercooked foodborne-illness warning is stripped before item matching and does not create egg, fish, or shellfish allergen signals.
The Jina Reader artifact is third-party transport corroboration only and is never relabeled as restaurant-issued evidence.
The frozen generic structured extraction promoted eighteen nested toppings, sauces, protein add-ons, and the KIDS heading into products and shifted 39 descriptions or categories.

The reviewed parser reads only top-level products under the twenty published category headings, excludes the Most Ordered duplicate rail, parses each official item anchor, and asserts 178 presentations and 175 canonical products.

Focused tests cover source shape, repeat canonicalization, every frozen-row disposition, raw-warning semantics, official-versus-third-party source separation, published repair invariants, and target-only idempotency.

sourceAttempts.outcome: Public web reader returned the live official page and confirmed Anthony's Restaurant at 3000 Annandale Rd, Falls Church, VA 22042.
Public web reader returned the current official Owner menu with all twenty category headings, 178 top-level category presentations, descriptions, prices, and item anchors.
HTTP 403 for the ledger archival user-agent; no artifact was recorded from the failed request.
Browser-control backend was unavailable, and direct curl with a browser-style user-agent also returned HTTP 403.
HTTP 200. Retained a complete 49,317-byte Markdown transport snapshot and cross-checked its source rows against the live official web-reader output.
Targeted review of the official menu and linked item surfaces found ingredient descriptions and a raw-food warning but no complete allergen matrix or cross-contact disclosure.

findings.summary: The frozen extraction promoted seventeen nested toppings/sauces/add-ons and the KIDS category heading into standalone products.
findings.resolution: Restricted parsing to top-level item anchors within published category sections and added source-shape and artifact regression assertions.

findings.summary: Nine current products—including Grape Leaves Dinner, Chicken Parmesan Dinner, Kids Hot Dog, four priced side proteins, With Meat Sauce, and Caesar dressing—were absent from the app.
findings.resolution: Restored all nine and published the complete 175-product canonical menu.

findings.summary: Eighty matched frozen products had missing or overstated signals relative to current official product and ingredient text.
findings.resolution: Recomputed positive signals from current product/ingredient text, preserved 46 unavailable rows, and added checks for Garidomakaronada and Tilapia Almandine.

findings.summary: The raw-food consumer warning mentions eggs, seafood, and shellfish but is not item-level allergen evidence and could smear those signals onto steak or kabob rows.
findings.resolution: Removed the warning before allergen matching and added a regression asserting that New York Steak retains no signal from the advisory.

findings.summary: Direct archival agents receive HTTP 403, requiring a transport mirror for a retained full-menu artifact; that mirror cannot be promoted to official authority.
findings.resolution: Anchored claims to the independently read official page/item URLs, labeled the mirror third-party, and kept the mirror out of app source URLs.

repairs.summary: Rebuilt Anthony's current menu and corrected modifier promotion, missing products, category shifts, allergen semantics, and source-authority boundaries.

notes: Frozen live target SHA-256 before repair: 72bba2a2108b7a2b7afd6010c7b6e712627227d0daff0f15ee44d23c2807c0ff.
Live non-target restaurant projection SHA-256 remained 4c5fba5863b1e869e412a6a2cef56a4556b680ab8f3dbd56939d87e05ac07689.
A second target-only repair run produced the same target hash, retained exactly one Anthony's repair note, and removed the classifier's false rejection of the priced WITH MEAT SAUCE product.
The repaired repository contains 113735 live menu items; the top-level historical itemCount field was intentionally not used as evidence.
